proof_boundary: This contract defines scoped legal/privacy/security approval evidence. It does not enable real uploads, create hosted users, approve AI/vendor processing, or approve public launch.

required_evidence_categories.label: Approval scope and data-flow map
required_evidence_categories.why: The reviewer must know the exact product scope, routes, vendors, data categories, and real-data boundary.

required_evidence_categories.label: Reviewer identity, qualification, and scope
required_evidence_categories.why: Legal/privacy/security approval requires named qualified reviewers and the exact scope they reviewed.

required_evidence_categories.label: Privacy notice, consent, and purpose review
required_evidence_categories.why: Customer-facing data collection needs clear purpose, consent, and notice language before real users or files.

required_evidence_categories.label: Collection minimization and retention review
required_evidence_categories.why: The product must limit collection, use, disclosure, and retention for trade documents and account data.

required_evidence_categories.label: Access, deletion, and challenge process
required_evidence_categories.why: Users need a reviewed process for access, correction, deletion, and challenging compliance.

required_evidence_categories.label: Breach response, recordkeeping, and notification
required_evidence_categories.why: A privacy/security incident process must exist before sensitive trade documents are accepted.

required_evidence_categories.label: Vendor, processor, and data-residency review
required_evidence_categories.why: Hosting, storage, AI, payment, email, monitoring, and support vendors need privacy/security review and location decisions.

required_evidence_categories.label: Managed auth, sessions, and RBAC review
required_evidence_categories.why: Real users need reviewed authentication, admin MFA, tenant authorization, session, and access-control proof.

required_evidence_categories.label: Upload, malware, and quarantine controls
required_evidence_categories.why: Real file uploads must have allowlists, type checks, size limits, quarantine, scanning/CDR decision, and storage isolation.

required_evidence_categories.label: AI redaction and prompt-injection review
required_evidence_categories.why: Uploaded documents and source text can carry sensitive data or prompt-injection content; AI use needs consent, redaction, and review.

required_evidence_categories.label: Secrets, logging, and monitoring review
required_evidence_categories.why: Production secrets, logs, alerts, and monitoring must avoid leaking trade documents, credentials, or personal information.

required_evidence_categories.label: Backup, restore, and incident-runbook review
required_evidence_categories.why: Operations must be able to recover, contain incidents, and notify owners before a real beta.

required_evidence_categories.label: Unresolved findings and risk acceptance
required_evidence_categories.why: Open findings need severity, owner, due date, and explicit risk acceptance before any scoped approval can count.

required_evidence_categories.label: Launch owner scope decision
required_evidence_categories.why: A named owner must decide whether the reviewed scope is approved, blocked, demo-only, or needs more evidence.

source_anchors.name: PIPEDA fair information principles
source_anchors.product_use: Accountability, purpose, consent, limiting collection/use/retention, safeguards, openness, access, and challenge evidence anchor.
source_anchors.publisher: Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada

source_anchors.name: Privacy breaches at your business
source_anchors.product_use: Breach response, recordkeeping, notification, and incident owner evidence anchor.
source_anchors.publisher: Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada

source_anchors.name: Baseline cyber security controls for small and medium organizations
source_anchors.product_use: Account, backup, patching, malware, monitoring, and incident-readiness control anchor.

source_anchors.name: Application Security Verification Standard
source_anchors.product_use: Application security review depth, authentication, authorization, validation, API, and deployment evidence anchor.

source_anchors.name: File Upload Cheat Sheet
source_anchors.product_use: Upload allowlisting, signature/type validation, generated filenames, size limits, authorization, isolated storage, malware checks, and CSRF anchor.

source_anchors.name: LLM01 Prompt Injection
source_anchors.product_use: AI prompt-injection risk anchor for uploaded files, web/source summaries, and reviewer-work-order drafting.

source_anchors.name: AI Risk Management Framework
source_anchors.product_use: AI trustworthiness, governance, evaluation, and human-review boundary anchor.

source_anchors.name: Cybersecurity Framework
source_anchors.product_use: Govern, identify, protect, detect, respond, and recover control grouping anchor.

source_anchors.name: Secure by Design
source_anchors.product_use: Secure-by-design/default posture and product-owner accountability anchor.
